question: Should The EU implement a common pension fund?

That way, every citizen would have an overview of his or her pension entitlements at all times, not just until when they are due.
negative_answer: The proposal assumes that pension entitlements are standardized enough to be aggregated into a single digital overview, but this ignores the fundamental reality of national fiscal sovereignty. Pension systems across the EU are built on vastly different actuarial models, contribution rates, and legal definitions of eligibility. Attempting to consolidate these heterogeneous data sets into one platform would result in inaccurate projections and systemic data errors. Rather than providing clarity, a centralized digital system would likely present a distorted and unreliable picture of actual future benefits due to the irreconcilable differences in how member states calculate and fund their respective social security obligations.
